title: viola acuminata var. brevistipulata
intro: Viola acuminata var. brevistipulata, also known as a variety of the common pansy, is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots are used to treat ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, with health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Viola acuminata var. brevistipulata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
